title: Merge Conflicts
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Merge Conflicts.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Code conflicts indicators analysis, manual resolution, and git merge continue.

content_sections.title: Merge Conflicts
content_sections.content:
### Semantic Markups, Specificity & Git Storage
Web interfaces require structural semantics, style precedence, and version control graphs.

#### Specificity Vectors
CSS determines style priority using a specificity vector `(Inline, ID, Class/Attribute/Pseudo-class, Element/Pseudo-element)`:
*   `#header .nav a` has specificity `(0, 1, 1, 1)`.
*   `div.container ul li` has specificity `(0, 0, 1, 3)`.
*   `!important` overrides specificity vectors, disrupting cascade rules. Avoid in production stylesheets.

#### Git Storage Internals
Git is a content-addressable storage system that models history as a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) of commit objects. Files are stored as **Blobs** compressed via zlib and keyed by their SHA-1 hash. **Trees** represent directories mapping filenames to Blob hashes. **Commits** point to parent commits, a root Tree object, and metadata (author, message).

#### Branch Merges
*   **Fast-Forward Merge:** If the target branch has no new commits, Git simply moves the branch pointer forward.
*   **Three-Way Merge:** If branches have diverged, Git finds the common base commit, creates a new merge commit, and resolves conflicts using three-way file diffs.

### Asymptotic and Spatial Complexity Analysis
We summarize the computational characteristics for Merge Conflicts implementations in the following reference matrix:

| Process/Operation | Worst-Case Time | Average-Case Time | Space Complexity |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Core Logic Execution | $O(N)$ | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ |
| Memory Operations | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(N)$ |

### Interactive Virtualization Configuration
To aid conceptual comprehension, the web client maps this topic to the `css-specificity-vector-calculator` simulation runtime, drawing state vectors from the following callout schema.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Merge Conflicts
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Merge Conflicts is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.
